clinical.presenting_complaints: Recurrent sore throat with high fever, 8-9 episodes per year for 3 years. Difficulty swallowing. Habitual snoring and mouth breathing at night.
clinical.initial_assessment: General Condition Conscious, cooperative, well-nourished child. Mouth breathing noted. Weight / Height 38 kg / 148 cm Vitals BP 104/66 mmHg, Pulse 84/min, SpO2 99% (room air), Temp 98.6 F, RR 18/min CVS S1 S2 heard, no murmurs Respiratory Air entry bilateral, clear Abdomen Soft, non-tender CNS No focal deficits Local — Oropharynx Bilateral tonsils Grade III enlargement, cryptic surface, congested. No peritonsillar bulge. Uvula midline. Posterior pharyngeal wall — mild granularity. Local — Nose Bilateral inferior turbinate hypertrophy. Nasal mucosa congested. Local — Ears Bilateral tympanic membranes intact. No middle ear effusion. Pre-operative Examination: BP 104/66 mmHg, Pulse 84/min, SpO2 99%, afebrile. Grade III bilateral tonsillar hypertrophy, cryptic and congested. X-ray neck lateral: adenoid-nasopharynx ratio 0.7 — adenoid hypertrophy confirmed.
clinical.significant_past_history: Recurrent tonsillitis since age 9. No other chronic illnesses. No known drug allergies. No previous surgeries.
clinical.primary_diagnosis_at_admission: Chronic Recurrent Tonsillitis, Grade III Bilateral Tonsillar Hypertrophy with Adenoid Hypertrophy — ICD-10: J35.0 / J35.3
clinical.final_diagnosis_at_discharge: Post-operative status following bilateral tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy for chronic recurrent tonsillitis with Grade III tonsillar hypertrophy and adenoid hypertrophy — ICD-10: J35.3
clinical.key_investigations: Haemoglobin (Hb), WBC Count, Platelet Count, Bleeding Time (BT), Clotting Time (CT), Blood Group, Blood Sugar Random, ECG, X-Ray Neck Lateral View.
clinical.investigation_findings: BP 104/66 mmHg, Pulse 84/min, SpO2 99%, afebrile. Grade III bilateral tonsillar hypertrophy, cryptic and congested. X-ray neck lateral: adenoid-nasopharynx ratio 0.7 — adenoid hypertrophy confirmed. Haemoglobin (Hb) 12.4 g/dL, WBC Count 8,600 /cumm, Platelet Count 2.8 Lakh, Bleeding Time (BT) 2 min 30 sec, Clotting Time (CT) 5 min 10 sec, Blood Group B Positive, Blood Sugar Random 88 mg/dL, ECG Normal sinus rhythm, X-Ray Neck Lateral View Enlarged adenoid shadow. Adenoid-nasopharynx ratio 0.7 (elevated). No retropharyngeal collection. Adenoid hypertrophy.
clinical.treatment_given:
Day 1 (26/06/2026): Comfortable post-op, vitals stable, IV fluids and IV analgesics. Throat pain expected and managed with IV Paracetamol and Tramadol.
Day 2 (27/06/2026): Afebrile, tolerating oral liquids, throat pain persisting — IV analgesics continued. No bleeding from operative site.
Day 3 (28/06/2026): Low-grade fever (99.2F), throat pain — clinical decision to extend stay by 1 day for IV antibiotics and observation. No signs of secondary haemorrhage.
Day 4 (29/06/2026): Afebrile, throat pain improving, tolerating soft diet, declared medically fit for discharge.
clinical.operative_findings: Bilateral Grade III tonsils — severely hypertrophied, cryptic, adherent to pillars. Adenoids: large, obstructing posterior choanae bilaterally — adenoidectomy clinically necessary. No intraoperative complications.
clinical.complications: Prolonged throat pain and low-grade fever on Day 3 necessitating 1-day extension of hospital stay. No secondary haemorrhage. No airway compromise.
clinical.advice_on_discharge: Medications: Tab. Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 375mg 1 tablet Twice daily 5 Days, Tab. Paracetamol 500mg 1 tablet Three times daily 5 Days, Tab. Ibuprofen 200mg 1 tablet Twice daily after food 3 Days, Syp. Pantoprazole 20mg 5 ml Once daily before breakfast 7 Days, Throat Gargles (Betadine diluted) As directed Twice daily 7 Days. Follow-up: Day 7 (06/07/2026): Post-operative wound check at ENT OPD. Day 14 (13/07/2026): Review — histopathology report collection. Diet: Cold soft diet for 2 weeks. Avoid hot, spicy, hard foods for 3 weeks. Activity: Rest at home for 1 week. Avoid strenuous activity for 3 weeks. No school for 10 days. Warning Signs: Report immediately for any fresh bleeding from mouth/nose, high fever (>101F), or difficulty breathing.
clinical.discharge_summary_text:
Apollo Speciality Hospital
21, Greams Road, Chennai - 600006, Tamil Nadu
Ph: 044-28293333 | Email: records@apollochennai.org
Hospital Reg No: TN/CHE/2003/00412 | PMJAY Empanelment ID: H001
DISCHARGE SUMMARY
Summary No: APH/DS/2026/TCNEW02 Date: 29/06/2026
SECTION 1 — HOSPITAL & PATIENT INFORMATION
Hospital Name Apollo Speciality Hospital
Address 21, Greams Road, Chennai - 600006, Tamil Nadu
PMJAY Empanelment ID H001
Patient Name Aditya Suresh
Age / Gender 12 Years / Male
Guardian Mr. Suresh Krishnamurthy (Father)
PMJAY Beneficiary ID P002
PMJAY Card No. TN-PMJAY-2024-002567
Address 34, Anna Nagar East, Chennai - 600102
SECTION 2 — ADMISSION & DISCHARGE DETAILS
Date & Time of Admission 25/06/2026 at 10:00 hrs
Date & Time of Discharge 29/06/2026 at 12:00 hrs
Total Duration of Stay 4 Days (pre-auth indicative: 3 Days)
Reason for Extended Stay Post-operative pain management — patient required additional IV analgesics and
observation on Day 3 due to throat pain and low-grade fever. Extended by 1 day on
clinical grounds.
Type of Admission Planned / Elective
Ward / Room Category General Ward
Treating Consultant Dr. Meenakshi Sundaram
Qualification MBBS, MS (ENT), DLO
Registration Number TN-MED-44821
Specialty ENT (Ear, Nose & Throat)
PMJAY Package Booked SL016A — Tonsillectomy (U/L tonsillectomy unilateral/bilateral)
Pre-Auth Reference PMJAY-PREAUTH-2026-TCNEW02-001
SECTION 3 — CLINICAL SUMMARY
Presenting Complaints:
Recurrent sore throat with high fever, 8-9 episodes per year for 3 years. Difficulty swallowing. Habitual snoring and
mouth breathing at night.
Relevant Medical History:
Recurrent tonsillitis since age 9. No other chronic illnesses. No known drug allergies. No previous surgeries.
Pre-operative Examination:
BP 104/66 mmHg, Pulse 84/min, SpO2 99%, afebrile. Grade III bilateral tonsillar hypertrophy, cryptic and congested.
X-ray neck lateral: adenoid-nasopharynx ratio 0.7 — adenoid hypertrophy confirmed.
SECTION 4 — DIAGNOSIS
Provisional Diagnosis (Admission) Chronic Recurrent Tonsillitis, Grade III Bilateral Tonsillar Hypertrophy with Adenoid
Hypertrophy — ICD-10: J35.0 / J35.3
Final Diagnosis (Discharge) Post-operative status following bilateral tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy for chronic
recurrent tonsillitis with Grade III tonsillar hypertrophy and adenoid hypertrophy —
ICD-10: J35.3
SECTION 5 — PROCEDURES PERFORMED
DEVIATION NOTE: Pre-authorisation was obtained for bilateral tonsillectomy (SL016A). During surgery under GA,
significant adenoid hypertrophy was confirmed on direct visualisation (consistent with pre-operative X-ray finding of
adenoid-nasopharynx ratio 0.7). Adenoidectomy was performed in the same sitting as it was clinically necessary to
address the obstructive adenoid tissue. This is an intraoperative clinical decision deviation from the pre-authorised
procedure.
Procedure 1 (Pre-authed) Bilateral Tonsillectomy — PMJAY Package SL016A
Procedure 2 (Additional) Adenoidectomy — performed intraoperatively, not separately pre-authed
Date of Surgery 26/06/2026 at 09:30 hrs
Duration 75 minutes (combined)
Anaesthesia General Anaesthesia — Dr. Kavitha Rajan, MD Anaesthesiology, Reg:
TN-MED-31045
Technique — Tonsillectomy Dissection and snare technique. Bilateral tonsils removed. Haemostasis achieved
with bipolar cautery.
Technique — Adenoidectomy Adenoid tissue curetted under direct vision. Haemostasis achieved. Specimens sent
for histopathology.
Intraoperative Findings Bilateral Grade III tonsils — severely hypertrophied, cryptic, adherent to pillars.
Adenoids: large, obstructing posterior choanae bilaterally — adenoidectomy clinically
necessary. No intraoperative complications.
SECTION 6 — POST-OPERATIVE COURSE
Day 1 (26/06/2026): Comfortable post-op, vitals stable, IV fluids and IV analgesics. Throat pain expected and
managed with IV Paracetamol and Tramadol.
Day 2 (27/06/2026): Afebrile, tolerating oral liquids, throat pain persisting — IV analgesics continued. No bleeding from
operative site.
Day 3 (28/06/2026): Low-grade fever (99.2F), throat pain — clinical decision to extend stay by 1 day for IV antibiotics
and observation. No signs of secondary haemorrhage.
Day 4 (29/06/2026): Afebrile, throat pain improving, tolerating soft diet, declared medically fit for discharge.
SECTION 7 — COMPLICATIONS
No intraoperative complications. Post-operative: prolonged throat pain and low-grade fever on Day 3 necessitating
1-day extension of hospital stay. No secondary haemorrhage. No airway compromise.
SECTION 8 — CONDITION AT DISCHARGE
Stable. Afebrile. Throat pain reducing. Tolerating soft diet. Operative sites healing well. No active bleeding.
SECTION 9 — DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS
# Medication Dose Frequency Duration
1 Tab. Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 375mg 1 tablet Twice daily 5 Days
2 Tab. Paracetamol 500mg 1 tablet Three times daily 5 Days
3 Tab. Ibuprofen 200mg 1 tablet Twice daily after food 3 Days
4 Syp. Pantoprazole 20mg 5 ml Once daily before breakfast 7 Days
5 Throat Gargles (Betadine diluted) As directed Twice daily 7 Days
SECTION 10 — FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS
Day 7 (06/07/2026): Post-operative wound check at ENT OPD.
Day 14 (13/07/2026): Review — histopathology report collection.
Diet: Cold soft diet for 2 weeks. Avoid hot, spicy, hard foods for 3 weeks.
Activity: Rest at home for 1 week. Avoid strenuous activity for 3 weeks. No school for 10 days.
Warning Signs: Report immediately for any fresh bleeding from mouth/nose, high fever (>101F), or difficulty
breathing.
SECTION 11 — DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED WITH THIS DISCHARGE SUMMARY
The following documents are available and submitted for PMJAY claim processing:
# Document Status
1 Discharge Summary (CAM Annexure 6 format) Available — this document
2 Operative Notes (bilateral tonsillectomy + adenoidectomy) Available
3 Pre-Anaesthesia Check-up Report (ASA Grade I, dated 24/06/2026) Available
4 Post-operative Clinical Photograph (operative site Day 1) Available
5 Intraoperative Clinical Photograph (tonsil and adenoid specimen) Available
6 Gross Specimen Photograph (removed tonsils and adenoid tissue) Available
7 Histopathology Report (tonsil and adenoid tissue — sent for HPE) Available
8 Pre-operative Blood Reports (CBC, BT/CT, Blood Group, Sugar, ECG) Available
9 X-Ray Neck Lateral View Report (adenoid hypertrophy confirmed) Available
10 Indoor Case Papers / Clinical Notes Available
11 Informed Consent Form (signed by parent/guardian) Available
12 PMJAY Beneficiary Card Copy Available
13 Patient Photo on Hospital Bed (admission) Available
14 Anaesthesia Record Available
15 Deviation Justification Letter — Adenoidectomy (intraoperative decision) Available
SECTION 12 — TREATING DOCTOR'S DECLARATION
I certify that the patient was under my care, all treatments were medically necessary, procedures were performed as
documented, and the extended stay was clinically warranted. The additional adenoidectomy was an intraoperative
clinical decision based on direct visualisation confirming significant obstructive adenoid hypertrophy consistent with
pre-operative imaging. All mandatory claim documents are enclosed.
Consultant ENT Surgeon PMAM (PM-JAY Arogya Mitra) Guardian / Attendant
Dr. Meenakshi Sundaram Ms. Rani Selvam Mr. Suresh Krishnamurthy (Father)
